summary: Matched four fighter functions across Kirby SpecialN and Yoshi SpecialHi translation units. The PR body claims all four reached 100%, and the decomp-dev bot report lists four new 100% matches totaling +484 matched-code bytes.

key_files.role: Matched ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim and ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C, replacing placeholder comments with C bodies.
Matched ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter and ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter, replacing placeholder comments with C bodies.

what_changed: Replaced `/// #ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim` with a body that checks `!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)`, changes Kirby to `ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi3`, and sets `pre_hitlag_cb`/`post_hitlag_cb` to pause/resume all effects.

Replaced `/// #ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C` with a body that calls `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, preserves `cur_anim_frame`, chooses motion id `0x190` or `0x201` depending on `fp2->fv.kb.hat.kind`, changes motion state, and sets `accessory4_cb`.
Replaced `/// #ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter` with a body that initializes Yoshi SpecialHi movement/fighter fields, changes to `ftYs_MS_SpecialHi`, sets `accessory4_cb`, and calls `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.
Replaced `/// #ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter` with an analogous airborne entry body using `ftYs_MS_SpecialAirHi`.
Changed-file metadata shows 2 files touched: 32 additions/2 deletions in Kirby SpecialN and 34 additions/2 deletions in Yoshi SpecialHi.

Automated report lists 4 new matches: `ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C` (+124 bytes), `ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim` (+120 bytes), `ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter` (+120 bytes), and `ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter` (+120 bytes), each from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Used existing typed fighter fields and callbacks such as `fp->fv.kb.hat.kind`, `fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x0`, `fp->throw_flags`, `fp->cmd_vars[0]`, and `fp->accessory4_cb` rather than raw offsets.
Kept unproven semantic names conservative: helper names like `fn_800F9260` and `fn_8012E110`, and unknown-looking fields such as `x0`, `x4`, and `x2238`, were not renamed in this slice.
For the two Yoshi entry functions, preserved the nearly identical source shape while only varying the motion-state constant for ground versus air.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small fighter state-transition functions may match cleanly by expressing the state change directly through existing fighter APIs and typed fighter fields, then restoring callback fields after `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` when the diff shows that order.

For this Yoshi SpecialHi pair, ground and air entry functions matched as duplicated sibling bodies with only the motion-state ID differing; this is evidence for preserving the sibling entry-function shape here, not a general rule to duplicate all similar code.

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names like `fp`, `fp2`, `gobj`, `motion_id`, and `anim_frame` where the role is clear.
Do not invent semantics for unresolved helper or field names; this PR leaves names such as `fn_800F9260`, `fn_8012E110`, `x0`, `x4`, and `x2238` intact.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Yoshi entry functions include a narrow local padding declaration `u8 _[24];` before field initialization.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is evidence that the tactic appeared in the matched PR slice, not enough by itself to make padding a general style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Yoshi entry functions first use `gobj->user_data` for `fp`, call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, then reload with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` before assigning `accessory4_cb`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Likely codegen-sensitive ordering, supported here by the 100% bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Kirby `ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C` assigns default `motion_id = 0x190`, conditionally changes it to `0x201`, and passes preserved `cur_anim_frame` into `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: A simple default-then-conditional assignment matched this function.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported GALE01 matched code increased to 56.01% (+0.01%, +484 bytes) with 4 new 100% matches.
No human review comments or reviews were included in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: fighter entry functions reload fp after Fighter_ChangeMotionState

notes: The PR identity, files, function names, changed code, merge time, and automated match report are present in the loaded slice.
The PR body explicitly says all four listed functions are 100% matched; the bot report independently lists the same four as new 100% matches.
No human review comments were available, so reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.
The slice does not include full surrounding files or objdiff logs beyond the bot report, so broader style conclusions should stay narrow.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Yoshi SpecialHi entry matches used padding and fighter reload
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2269, `ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter` and `ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter` both matched with `u8 _[24];`, initialization of Yoshi SpecialHi fields, `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, then `fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` before assigning `accessory4_cb` and calling `ftAnim_8006EBA4`. Keep this record scoped to these functions unless further evidence supports generalization.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Kirby SpecialNLg matched with typed hat-kind branch selecting motion id
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2269, `ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C` matched by reading `fp2->fv.kb.hat.kind`, selecting motion id `0x190` or `0x201`, preserving `fp->cur_anim_frame`, then changing motion state and setting `accessory4_cb`.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `u8 _[24]` padding to a general standard from this PR alone; it is only shown as part of two matched Yoshi functions.
Do not infer human reviewer preferences; the loaded slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.
Do not infer that duplicated sibling bodies are generally preferred over helper extraction; the evidence only supports the exact matched Yoshi ground/air entry functions in this PR.
